title: Tool
description: A named callable capability an agent or Model Context Protocol (MCP) host can invoke while doing factory work.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: A tool is a named callable capability an agent or Model Context Protocol (MCP) host can invoke while doing work. Calling a tool means asking for a concrete action by name—read a file, run a command, or call a Factory Session tool such as you.factory_session.validate_source. The tool is that callable action, not the factory that schedules work and not the harness that runs the agent.

whyItMatters.title: Why It Matters
whyItMatters.body: Without tools, agents and hosts mostly produce text. With tools, they can inspect a project, validate a workflow, start a session, or change work in the factory and on the machine. That is how factory work moves from advice into actions you can check and repeat.

simpleExample.title: Simple Example
simpleExample.body: An MCP host can call a Factory Session tool such as you.factory_session.validate_source. Scaffold copy — a compact concrete example lands in a later story.

commonConfusions.title: Common Confusions
commonConfusions.body: A tool is not the harness, not MCP host-setup documentation, and not a worker. Scaffold copy — full confusion boundaries land in a later story.
